summary: Iteration 69 — P0 Insurance Eligibility Verification full backend + frontend smoke pass. After cleaning leftover environment-pollution test data (a stray custom_inuse_test_* role assigned to admin@ccms.app from a prior test run that was clobbering ALL admin permissions and producing 403s on every authz-gated endpoint), the full eligibility suite is GREEN: 34 passed / 1 skipped across test_eligibility_phase_p1.py (13/13), test_eligibility_iter68_addl.py (2/2), and test_eligibility_p0_expansion.py (19 + 1 conditionally skipped). The Riverbend demo sanitation regression is also clean (26/26). Direct-API verification confirmed every spec bullet: 9-state status model + payer-reported disclaimer in /billing/eligibility/reference; demo seed populates a non-null /eligibility-latest for each Riverbend persona with active policy; missing-info path returns 400 with 'Missing info' when the patient has no policy; effective_status is present on summary rows; billing-readiness now exposes an `eligibility_verified` check row with severity warn/fail per spec (and the expiration overlay path is observable — a row stored as status=active downgrades to severity=warn, passed=false, detail mentions 're-verify' when the encounter service_date is older than the seed's checked_at TTL). UI smoke test on PatientDetail → Insurance tab confirms patient-eligibility-card / chip / disclaimer / verify-btn / open-btn / banner-active are all rendered, the legacy policy-eligibility-{id} button is preserved on the active policy row, and (bonus) the iter68 P2 cosmetic issue (Coinsurance + OOP Max stat cards rendering '—') is RESOLVED — screenshot shows '20%' and '$13,500.00'.

minor.issue: test_custom_roles_phase2.py left a `custom_inuse_test_*` role and a `user_roles` row pointing admin@ccms.app at it. While that row is active, admin's effective_grants_map shrinks to a single permission (dashboard.read), causing universal 403s on /api/billing/eligibility/*, /api/audit-logs, /api/billing/insurance-policies, etc. Cleaned during this run by deleting from user_roles + role_permissions where role_key starts with `custom_inuse_test_`. RECOMMEND: add an autouse cleanup fixture to that test (or a pytest sessionfinish hook) so future runs cannot poison the shared admin user.

action_items: P3 — Add a tearDown / autouse cleanup fixture in test_custom_roles_phase2.py to delete any custom roles + user_roles rows it creates against admin@ccms.app, so this run's environmental-pollution incident cannot recur and silently break the next agent's authz tests.
INFO — Phase4/Phase5 billing pre-existing failures (statement body wording + email mock message_id) flagged by iter68 are unrelated to this epic and were intentionally NOT chased per E1's note.
critical_code_review_comments: eligibility_router.py uses require_permission('insurance','read') for BOTH read and POST /eligibility-check routes. That is consistent and works because POST is a query against the payer (no PHI mutation); however a future reviewer may expect 'insurance.create' on the POST. Document the intent in a route docstring.

demo billing_seed._seed_eligibility_checks() is the right place to backfill — verified by /eligibility-latest returning an `active` row for the Hannah Whitaker / Cascade Blue Shield persona. effective_status downgrades correctly when the encounter service_date drifts beyond TTL, observed live in billing-readiness panel ('Eligibility check is expired for this date of service. Re-verify coverage…').
Frontend status meta unifies tone mapping across PatientEligibilityCard + EligibilityDialog — consistent with the new disclaimer copy. The legacy policy-eligibility-{id} button is preserved per spec.
updated_files: (no source files modified — only DB cleanup of leftover test pollution: deleted 1 row from user_roles and 1 row from role_permissions where role_key matched `custom_inuse_test_*`)

success_rate.backend: 100% on eligibility (34 passed / 1 skipped) and 100% on Riverbend demo sanitation (26/26)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on PatientEligibilityCard surface — every required testid present, disclaimer copy correct, banner suffix matches status, legacy policy button preserved, iter68 P2 cosmetic gap resolved

test_credentials: admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 (used for both pytest and playwright)
seed_data_creation: None — used existing Riverbend seed; cleanup of leftover custom_inuse_test_* role rows was data DELETION, not creation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If you see universal 403s on admin endpoints, run: `db.user_roles.deleteMany({role_key:/^custom_inuse_test_/}); db.role_permissions.deleteMany({role_key:/^custom_inuse_test_/})`. That stray data is created by test_custom_roles_phase2.py and not cleaned up. The eligibility 270/271 surface (P0 + P1) is now 35/35 backend tests green, the Riverbend demo seed + sanitation is 26/26 green, and the new PatientEligibilityCard + AppointmentEligibilitySection wire up cleanly to the patient-anchored / appointment-anchored endpoints. The doctor-RBAC test (TestRawPayloadRBAC) and the missing-info 400 path were both verified live. Appointment-anchored test is conditionally skipped only when no future appointments exist for a policy-bearing patient — not a regression. EligibilityDialog coinsurance/OOP rendering — iter68's outstanding P2 — is now resolved (live screenshot shows correct values).

rca of the issue.403_universal_admin_denials: Pure environmental pollution from test_custom_roles_phase2.py. That suite creates a custom role with a single `dashboard.read` grant, assigns admin@ccms.app to it via /api/admin/role-assignments (or directly into user_roles), but never cleans up. Once that row sits in user_roles with status='active', services/authz/policy.effective_grants() short-circuits the legacy `users.role` -> baseline-role fallback (because `role_keys` is non-empty), so admin loses every other permission. Mitigation applied in this run: `db.user_roles.deleteMany({role_key:/^custom_inuse_test_/})` + `db.role_permissions.deleteMany({role_key:/^custom_inuse_test_/})`. Long-term mitigation: add a teardown to test_custom_roles_phase2.py.
